page: Amazon Web Services m9g.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processor based on the arm64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores with a clock speed of 2.8 GHz, 256.0 GB of system memory, and a 34 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth. Multi-core stress-ng tests and bzip3 decompression show top-tier results, supported by a 48 MB L3 cache. Memory bandwidth tests, including both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads and writes, consistently rank in the top tier. Database operations and Redis SET operations also achieve top-tier throughput, with Redis reaching 183.27 million operations per second.

While multi-core and memory throughput are highly efficient, single-core CPU performance and cryptographic operations fall into the average tier. Additionally, LLM text generation benchmarks indicate poor performance for small models, reflecting a limitation in sequential AI inference tasks without dedicated GPU hardware. This hardware profile is suited for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, static web serving, and memory-intensive general-purpose applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by the arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 256.0 GB of system memory and a 34 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high database throughput, with Redis SET operations reaching 183.27 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation speeds are limited. This resource configuration is suited for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, and static web serving.

tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for database and multi-threaded workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
34 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
